summary_one_line: Input must carry the ASCII colorspace signature 'Lab ' in the ICC profile header so the dispatch switch hits case PT_Lab.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The 4-byte colorspace signature field of the ICC profile header (input offsets 0x10-0x16, rendered 'Lab ') must spell the Lab signature so T_COLORSPACE decodes to PT_Lab and the switch in _cmsICCcolorSpace selects case PT_Lab at cmspcs.c:781; loser inputs carrying 'RGB ' / 'XYZ ' fall through to non-Lab cases.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All winner-resolving seeds carry the literal bytes 4c 61 62 20 ('Lab ') at offsets 0x10-0x13 (and again at 0x14-0x16), so the parsed colorspace decodes to PT_Lab and the true side of the case PT_Lab branch is taken (W exclusively hits source line 781).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed instead holds 52 47 42 20 / 58 59 5a 20 ('RGB ' / 'XYZ ') at those same offsets, so the switch never reaches PT_Lab; L takes the default arm at line 805 and leaves line 781 unhit (T=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace intercepts the equality comparison of the parsed colorspace tag against the Lab constant, logs both operands, and splices the Lab signature bytes into the input at the matching offset; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace so they must blindly mutate a 4-byte ASCII tag (effectively a magic-value match) by chance, which never happens in 10 trials. The mechanism is identical for both decisive pairs, hence single_feature.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of the case PT_Lab switch arm in _cmsICCcolorSpace.
Only the winner-resolving seed set hits the blocker source line, while loser falls to the default arm.
The loser-blocking seeds take the default switch arm instead of PT_Lab.
The switch operand is the colorspace decoded from the input format in OptimizeByResampling.
Winner seeds carry the ASCII 'Lab Lab ' colorspace bytes in the ICC header.
Loser seeds carry 'RGB XYZ ' at the same colorspace offsets.
The colorspace-signature offsets are exactly where W and L bytes fully differ (L vs R, a vs G, b vs B).
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a logged CMP operand into the input at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so the vpc>vp delta is also attributable to I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2243 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0aedfccd0898c3ff`; the ancestor chain has 17 levels and the trailing I2S-floor signal reports 1 dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 16, the ParentInfo-only signature of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a 4-byte ASCII colorspace tag where naive/value_profile resolve the Lab case as often as cmplog/vpc within the same trial budget would refute I2S as the decisive mechanism.

weakest_evidence_point: The dash-row I2S-floor signal is a lower bound and the literal I2SRandReplace string is not logged, so the depth-16 row is corroborating rather than conclusive proof the Lab-tag substitution itself came from I2S.
